scope_note: QA Inverse Galois Degree-24 Cert [506]. CLAIM: QA sigma on Cosmos sub-orbit concretely realizes C24 as a degree-24 transitive subgroup of S24 — connecting to SAIR IGP24 competition (Tao et al. 2026, 165836 degree-24 Galois group pairs). (IG_1) sigma restricted to any Cosmos sub-orbit is a 24-cycle in S24: orbit length=24, sigma^24(1,1)=(1,1), sigma^k(1,1)!=(1,1) for 0<k<24; (IG_2) regular representation: 0 fixed points of sigma^k (0<k<24) across all 3 Cosmos sub-orbits; (IG_3) exactly 3 Cosmos sub-orbits of length 24 = 72/24 = 3 = |C3| coset count from CRT C24 = C3 x C8; (IG_4) CRT decomposition: sigma^8 period 3, sigma^3 period 8, gcd(3,8)=1, 3*8=24. Theorem NT: Galois group characters (roots of unity, splitting fields, Magma GaloisGroup()) are observer projections over discrete sigma-orbit structure. NOT CLAIMED: specific polynomial over Q with Gal(f/Q)=C24; LMFDB T-number disambiguation; degree-24 transitive group enumeration beyond C24. Companion: [128] pi(9)=24, [499] all-initializations, [504] Star-G tensor CRT. Primary: Tao T. et al. (2026) SAIR IGP24 competition; Wildberger N.J. (2005) ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8. Derived 2026-06-23.
